name: Mózar — circular by the Iglesia and the río Tera
name_original: Mózar — circular por la Iglesia y el río Tera
region_intro: Hiking around Villanázar offers varied terrain, characterized by the presence of significant rivers such as the Eria, Órbigo, and Tera, which shape the landscape with their banks and valleys. The region also features historical routes like the Vía de la Plata and greenways, alongside gentle hills and viewpoints. These elements combine to create a diverse environment for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Villanázar The most popular hiking route is Basilia's Balcony and Coto, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete, offering views from elevated points. Another top favourite among local hikers is Vía de la Plata and Vía Verde del Oeste Loop, a moderate 6.9 miles (11.1 km) path. This route combines historical pathways with greenway sections, providing a mix of cultural and natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Banks of the Eria and Órbigo Trail, a 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Hiking around Villanázar is defined by riverbanks, historical paths, and gentle hills. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels, with 10 easy and 12 moderate routes available. The routes in Villanázar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 50 hikers have used komoot to explore Villanázar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Villanázar?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Villanázar, offering a variety of experiences. You'll find a good mix of easy and moderate routes, with 10 easy and 12 moderate options to choose from.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Villanázar?
A: Yes, Villanázar offers 10 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306783109/mozar-circular-by-the-iglesia-and-the-rio-tera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mózar — circular by the Iglesia and the río Tera&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 2.8 miles (4.5 km) long and takes around 1 hour 10 minutes to complete.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Villanázar?
A: Hiking around Villanázar is characterized by varied terrain, including riverbanks along the Eria, Órbigo, and Tera rivers, historical pathways like the Vía de la Plata, and greenways. You'll also encounter gentle hills and viewpoints, offering a diverse natural and cultural landscape.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Villanázar area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305317182/via-de-la-plata-and-via-verde-del-oeste-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata and Vía Verde del Oeste Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.9-mile (11.1 km) path that combines historical routes with greenway sections. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022509543/pena-redonda-and-miradores-del-coto-and-de-las-suertes-circular-by-the-valles-de-benavente-from-morales-de-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Redonda and miradores del Coto and de las Suertes — circular by the Valles de Benavente from Morales de Rey&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails around Villanázar?
A: The region offers several interesting sights. You can explore historical bridges such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2810181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Esla River&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Azoague Iron Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2720898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María del Azogue&lt;/a&gt; are also nearby points of interest.

Q: Are there any hikes that follow riverbanks in Villanázar?
A: Yes, the region is shaped by significant rivers, and you can find trails that follow their banks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022487025/banks-of-the-eria-and-orbigo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Banks of the Eria and Órbigo Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.3-mile (13.4 km) route that leads through beautiful riverine landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Villanázar?
A: The trails in Villanázar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, which includes riverbanks, historical paths, and gentle hills, making for enjoyable and diverse outdoor experiences.

Q: Are there any trails with good viewpoints in Villanázar?
A: Yes, some trails offer elevated points with good views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305941795/basilia-s-balcony-and-coto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilia's Balcony and Coto&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, is a moderate 4.9-mile (7.8 km) trail specifically noted for offering views from elevated positions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Villanázar?
A: The region's climate generally allows for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes ideal. Winter hiking is also possible, though conditions may vary.

Q: Are there any historical routes to explore in Villanázar?
A: Yes, Villanázar is home to historical routes such as sections of the Vía de la Plata. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305317182/via-de-la-plata-and-via-verde-del-oeste-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata and Vía Verde del Oeste Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to experience these ancient pathways combined with modern greenway sections.

Q: What are the typical durations for hikes in Villanázar?
A: Hikes in Villanázar vary in duration. Easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306783109/mozar-circular-by-the-iglesia-and-the-rio-tera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mózar — circular by the Iglesia and the río Tera&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in just over an hour, while moderate routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022487025/banks-of-the-eria-and-orbigo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Banks of the Eria and Órbigo Trail&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 3 hours 23 minutes.
tips: A beautiful and charming church in the small town of Mózar on the banks of the Tera River.
The Tera River is the setting for this incredible outdoor natural park. You can find riverside hiking trails and even a river beach to soak in on a hot summer day or go fishing.
